evals.prompt: Kurze Frage für die Runde: Neues Greenfield-Postgres-Schema, Spalte created_at — nehme ich TIMESTAMPTZ oder TIMESTAMP? Aktuell eine Region, plane Multi-Region nächstes Jahr. Kontext ist komplett auf dem Tisch, keine Altlasten, kein Framework-Zwang. Was sagt der Rat?
evals.expected_output: This is a sharp, narrow question: one Hersteller source (PostgreSQL docs), aligned community position, no genuine tension between technical and operational framing, single decision variable. The Council MUST produce a single compressed verdict turn — not seven turns. Shape: (1) brief grounding in one-to-two sentences naming PostgreSQL docs and community consensus, with a visible WebSearch call if web access available; (2) five MECE angles in prose named by role (e.g. 'Abhängigkeitsseitig (Kartograph) …', 'Argumentativ (Analyst) …', 'Was es kaputt macht (Adversary) …', 'Was draußen steht (Scout) …', 'Operativ …'), each one to three sentences; (3) verdict sentence naming the recommendation and the condition under which it would change; (4) first step in one sentence. Message ends with the first step. No steering menu, no sentinel. FAILURE MODES this test catches: running all seven turns ('ceremony for a sharp question'), adding em-dash steering menus, using `## Role · N/7` headers, skipping grounding, omitting any of the five role-angles, missing the first-step sentence.

evals.prompt: Wir denken darüber nach, unsere Auth-Bibliothek aus dem Monolithen in einen eigenen HTTP-Service zu ziehen. Sechs Dienste importieren heute aus lib/auth. Letztes Quartal gab es einen 3-Stunden-Ausfall, als wir das Token-Format geändert haben und Notifications auf einer älteren Version gepinnt waren. Admin-Impersonation stellt außerdem immer noch Full-Scope-Tokens aus. Sollen wir dieses Quartal, nächstes Quartal oder gar nicht extrahieren? Was sagt der Rat?
evals.expected_output: Broad, multi-dimensional strategic question with genuine tension (technical complexity vs management pressure vs security debt vs release risk). Full seven-turn run is correct here. Critical shape checks for the new advisor-voice contract: (a) NO turn headers — no '## Cartographer · 2/7', no '## Analyst · 3/7'; the role identifies itself inside the first sentence of its prose ('Als Kartograph schaue ich zuerst darauf …', 'Ich bin der Analyst …', 'Aus der Scout-Perspektive …'). (b) NO steering menu after T2-T6 — no em-dash sentinel like '— Cartographer fertig. Weiter: Analyst · oder widersprich · vertiefe · stopp'. Each turn ends where the advisor's thought ends. (c) T1's close DOES contain the steering primer in prose — a paragraph introducing 'weiter', 'widersprich', 'vertiefe', 'abzweig', 'stopp' in natural sentences. (d) T1 grounding: visible WebSearch/WebFetch calls if web access, Hersteller + Community + Divergence named in prose. (e) T3-T6 each engage with at least one prior role by name inside prose, not in a labeled 'Cross-reference (Pflicht):' block. (f) T7 Verdict: two paragraphs (broad question with real tension) — one operative/technical, one for decision-makers — as prose with role findings woven in by natural reference, ending with a first-step sentence. No MECE-Prüfung block, no citations list, no new Chairman findings. (g) Persistence line at end of T7 in environments with file access. FAILURE MODES this test catches: turn headers, steering menus after any turn except T1's close, labeled ceremony blocks, reasserted grounding in T7, template-shaped verdict instead of question-shaped verdict.

evals.prompt:
[This eval tests mid-run steering. The subagent running this test must produce a transcript simulating the following exchange, with each of the Council's messages being a separate turn.]

User T0: 'Frage an den Rat: Wir wollen unser Monitoring-Stack von Datadog auf eine self-hosted Prometheus+Grafana-Kombi migrieren, um Kosten zu sparen. Drei Teams, 40 Services, aktuell 30k/Jahr Datadog-Spend. Worth it?'

[Council runs T1 Read-back+Grounding. User replies 'weiter'.]
[Council runs T2 Cartographer. User replies 'weiter'.]
[Council runs T3 Analyst.]

User after T3: 'widersprich dem Analyst — er unterschätzt den Aufwand für Alerting-Rewrites, wir haben über 200 benutzerdefinierte Alerts in Datadog'

[Council must now produce the next assistant message, which is a re-run of the Analyst with the user's counter as additional input — NOT the scheduled T4 Adversary, because 'widersprich <rolle>' reruns that role. The next message should be the Analyst speaking again, taking the counter seriously.]
evals.expected_output: The critical test is the FOURTH assistant message — the Council's response to 'widersprich dem Analyst'. It must: (a) be the Analyst speaking again (not Adversary, not Chairman, not a meta-announcement) — opening with in-prose self-identification like 'Nochmal als Analyst — der Punkt mit den 200 Alerts trifft; ich korrigiere.' or 'Ich ziehe zurück. Bei 200 Custom-Alerts …'. (b) engage substantively with the counter: either correct the earlier derivation, defend it with a reason, or name the specific condition under which the counter changes the conclusion. (c) NOT re-announce the steering vocabulary in any form — no em-dash menu, no bullet list, no 'Wenn du weiter willst …' sentinel. The user already knows the vocabulary from T1. (d) NOT interpret the steering move as its own meta-paragraph ('Interpretation: widersprich → Analyst rerun → proceeding'); just do it. (e) NOT use a turn header like '## Analyst · 3/7 (rerun)'. Across all four messages (T1, T2, T3, Analyst-rerun): zero turn headers, zero em-dash steering menus, steering primer appears exactly once (end of T1 in prose). FAILURE MODES this test catches: menu repetition after every turn (condescension), meta-announcements of steering interpretation, confusing 'widersprich <role>' with a generic 'next' command and running the Adversary instead, turning the rerun into an Adversary-style attack instead of an Analyst-shaped re-examination.

The Council should NOT run all seven turns on a random assumption, and should NOT run the compressed verdict either (the question is too ambiguous for any verdict). Correct shape: a single diagnostic T1 that (a) names the missing dimension ('Es fehlt mir das Objekt — über was soll ich schauen?'), (b) offers two concrete interpretations to choose between, (c) states which default assumption would otherwise apply. No turn header, no em-dash steering menu, no seven-turn ceremony. The steering primer may appear in this T1's close since it is still a T1. Not multiple nested questions — one diagnostic message. This tests the upward-triage edge case: when the question is too ambiguous even for T1-plus-six, the right move is one diagnostic turn, not a pretend-deliberation.

evals.prompt:
Frage an den Rat: Für ein neues internes Tool wollen wir TypeScript strict mode von Anfang an aktivieren. Team ist drei Leute, kommen aus Python-Background, kein TypeScript-Vorwissen. Lohnt sich der strict-mode-Einstieg sofort oder erst nach ein paar Wochen Einarbeitung? Keine Zeit-Deadline, das Tool ist ein internes Dashboard.

[Die Arbeits-Directory enthält keine GROUNDING-CACHE.md zum Thema. Das Skill muss prüfen, ob die Datei existiert, feststellen dass kein Eintrag vorliegt, normalen WebSearch-basierten Grounding-Flow durchführen, und am Ende des Runs einen neuen Eintrag in GROUNDING-CACHE.md anlegen.]
evals.expected_output: This tests the Grounding-Cache-WRITE path. Required observable behavior: (a) T1 must reference GROUNDING-CACHE.md — either by attempting to read it, or by acknowledging its absence or empty state. This can be a single sentence in prose ('Ich schaue im GROUNDING-CACHE nach — kein Eintrag zum Thema.') or a visible file-read call. (b) Because no cache hit exists, T1 must proceed with a visible WebSearch / WebFetch call as usual. No skipping the grounding search. (c) T1's grounding block names Hersteller / Community / Divergence in the standard shape. No cache-origin sentence ('Grounding aus GROUNDING-CACHE.md …') appears, because this is a fresh fetch. (d) At T7 or immediately after, the run writes a new entry to GROUNDING-CACHE.md in the working directory, with the schema from the SKILL.md: `## <date> — <topic-slug>`, Hersteller / Community / Divergence, and the source hits list. The topic-slug should be substantive (e.g. `typescript-strict-mode-onboarding` or similar), not a verbatim echo of the question. (e) The run is otherwise shape-correct: this is a medium-complexity question that may run seven turns or a compressed verdict depending on the skill's shape judgment — either is acceptable; the cache-write behavior is what is under test. FAILURE MODES this test catches: skipping the cache read entirely, writing the cache but in wrong schema, writing the cache to the wrong location, omitting the topic-slug, leaving the cache empty after the run, rendering a cache-origin sentence on what is actually a fresh fetch.

evals.prompt:
Frage an den Rat: Für ein neues internes Tool wollen wir TypeScript strict mode von Anfang an aktivieren. Team ist drei Leute, kommen aus Python-Background, kein TypeScript-Vorwissen. Lohnt sich der strict-mode-Einstieg sofort oder erst nach ein paar Wochen Einarbeitung? Keine Zeit-Deadline, das Tool ist ein internes Dashboard.

[Die Arbeits-Directory enthält bereits eine GROUNDING-CACHE.md mit einem passenden, frischen Eintrag zum Thema TypeScript strict mode, geschrieben vor 2 Tagen. Das Skill muss den Cache-Hit erkennen, die WebSearch überspringen, und den Grounding-Block aus dem Cache rendern.]

files.content:
# Grounding-Cache — Council

## 2026-04-19 — typescript-strict-mode-onboarding

**Hersteller:** TypeScript Handbook (Microsoft, v5.4+), section 'Strict Type Checking Options' — strict mode is the recommended default for new projects; bundles seven individual checks including noImplicitAny and strictNullChecks.
**Community:** Major production reports (GitHub engineering blog 2025, Stripe TypeScript adoption writeup) consistently recommend starting strict from day one; retrofitting strict mode later is cited as the single most expensive migration TypeScript teams undertake.
**Divergence:** Aligned on 'strict from the start' for new codebases. Minor tension around null-checking ergonomics for teams new to static typing, but no substantive Hersteller/Community divergence.

**Source hits (for audit):**
- TypeScript Handbook — https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/tsconfig-json.html — strict mode recommended as default for new projects
- GitHub Engineering — https://github.blog/engineering/typescript-at-github/ — starting strict avoids a retrofit that costs more than the onboarding friction
- Stripe Engineering — https://stripe.com/blog/migrating-to-typescript — retrofitting strict is the most expensive TypeScript migration Stripe encountered

evals.expected_output: This tests the Grounding-Cache-HIT path. Required observable behavior: (a) T1 must read GROUNDING-CACHE.md — either a visible file-read tool call, or a prose acknowledgment that clearly demonstrates the cache was read ('Ich lese im GROUNDING-CACHE nach — Eintrag vom 2026-04-19 zum Thema TypeScript strict mode, passt.'). (b) T1 must NOT issue any WebSearch or WebFetch calls for this topic. The whole point of the cache hit is that fresh search is skipped. If a WebSearch appears in T1, this test fails. (c) T1's grounding block renders in the standard Hersteller / Community / Divergence shape, reusing the content from the cache entry. The block must close with one sentence signaling cache origin, for example 'Grounding aus GROUNDING-CACHE.md vom 2026-04-19, unverändert gültig.' or equivalent phrasing. (d) No duplicate entry is written to GROUNDING-CACHE.md at T7 — the cache hit does not trigger a fresh write. (e) The rest of the run proceeds normally (seven turns or compressed, whichever the shape judgment selects). FAILURE MODES this test catches: ignoring the cache and searching anyway (wastes the point), rendering the cache block without the origin-signal sentence (user cannot tell fresh from cached), writing a duplicate entry at T7 (pollutes the cache), mis-reading a fresh cache as stale and re-fetching.
